element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to keep ball in play and hit targets
Defeat enemies by hitting targets with pinball

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early gameplay (implied from Black Market availability)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Earned during gameplay, spent at Black Market for upgrades
Purchased to improve pinball control capability
Purchased to improve ball retention and platform control
New enemy type requiring established pinball combat skills
Obstacles and challenges beyond enemy combat
Stronger enemy type requiring upgraded equipment
Late-game enemies requiring full mastery of mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounter (Beezelbub, Dark Lord of the Machine)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final challenge culminating all learned skills

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to Black Market to purchase upgrades
Progressing to next stage after boss defeat
typical_tension_triggers: Ball in danger of leaving playfield
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation through six stages with acquisition-and-application cycles: earn gold during combat, visit Black Market for upgrades, face harder enemies with better equipment. Tension builds incrementally across stages (Tomb → Gate → Goblins → River → Harpy → Throne room) with environmental complexity increasing alongside enemy difficulty.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Black Market side area accessible during progression for upgrade purchasing (represents optional economic engagement)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to pinball mechanics through basic enemy encounters (skeletons) in Tomb; low equipment requirements
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy variety (demon dogs, goblins, environmental hazards) in Gate, Goblins, and River stages; players accumulate gold and make equipment choices
difficulty_curve.late_game: Damage-dealing harpies, demon guards, and dark knights in Harpy stage; final confrontation with Beezelbub requires fully upgraded flippers and stoppers
notable_spikes: River stage (Stage 4): introduction of environmental hazards alongside dark knights
Harpy stage (Stage 5): coordinated demon guard encounters
Throne room (Stage 6): Beezelbub boss fight requiring mastery of all mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Six-stage linear castle descent rescuing Princess Bali. Each stage introduces new enemy types and environmental hazards while maintaining core pinball-combat loop. Stages are thematically distinct (Tomb, Gate, Goblins, River, Harpy, Throne room) with escalating challenge.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All six RPG mode stages follow this pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Players earn gold during stage combat → visit Black Market to purchase flipper/stopper upgrades → return to combat with enhanced capabilities. This creates an RPG progression layer atop pinball mechanics.

Simple enemies (skeletons) in early stages evolve to coordinated multi-enemy encounters (goblin kids + goblin guards) and boss-level threats (Beezelbub). Each stage typically introduces one new enemy type.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tomb (skeletons) → Gate (demon dogs) → Goblins (goblins) → River (dark knights) → Harpy (harpies) → Throne room (Beezelbub)
